data.title: C3.ai Stock Gains on Landing Key FedRAMP Authorization
data.description:
C3.ai (AI) has achieved a major milestone with its newly granted FedRAMP authorization. This will allow the company to deliver its AI software suite to a wide range of U.S. federal agencies that handle sensitive data. The approval marks a major step forward in C3.ai's government strategy and boosts its position in the rapidly growing public‑sector AI market. Following the news, AI stock gained about 2% on Thursday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

What FedRAMP Authorization Means for C3.ai

FedRAMP, or the Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program, is the U.S. government's standardized framework for evaluating the security of cloud products and services. Obtaining this authorization is tough as companies must prove they meet strict rules for cybersecurity, data protection, and operational standards.

Only a limited number of AI-focused companies have achieved FedRAMP authorization. This positions C3.ai as a trusted provider at a time when agencies are accelerating AI adoption for defense, intelligence, logistics, and cybersecurity missions.

Moreover, FedRAMP approval often boosts confidence among enterprise clients in regulated industries such as energy, financial services, and healthcare. This could mean more revenue for the company.

Is C3.ai Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, C3.ai stock has a Hold consensus rating based on one Buy, five Hold, and three Sell ratings. The average C3.ai price target of $15.43 implies 4.1% downside risk from current levels. Year-to-date, AI stock has lost over 53%.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Eli Lilly's (LLY) Newest Weight-Loss Drug Delivers Strong Results in Late-Stage Trial
data.description:
Pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY) has announced that its newest obesity drug called Retatrutide delivered what appears to be the highest weight loss yet in a late-stage clinical trial.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

As an added benefit, the next-generation obesity medication also reduced knee pain due to arthritis. The strong trial results are the first of several studies planned for the weekly injection. Eli Lilly said that the highest dose of the drug helped patients with obesity and a type of knee arthritis lose an average of 23.7% of their body weight at 68 weeks.

The company added that some patients lost so much weight that they dropped out of the clinical trial. When evaluating only patients who stayed on the drug, the highest dose delivered an average weight loss of 28.7%. LLY stock is up 4% on Dec. 11 following news of the trial results.

A More Effective Weight-Loss Drug

This is the first late-stage data Eli Lilly has released on Retatrutide, which works differently from existing weight-loss injections and appears to be more effective. Eli Lilly is betting on Retatrutide as the next phase of its obesity portfolio after its weight-loss injection Zepbound and its upcoming pill called Orforglipron.

However, it remains unclear when Eli Lilly's newest weight-loss drug could hit the market. It's a critical part of the drug maker's plan to maintain its market share and lead over archrival Novo Nordisk (NVO) in the market for obesity and diabetes drugs. Some analysts estimate that weight-loss drugs could generate annual revenues of $100 billion or more by 2030.

Retatrutide also met the trial's other main goal of reducing knee pain caused by arthritis, a common condition that wears down the joint's cartilage and leads to pain and stiffness. The drug reduced knee pain in trial participants by an average of 62.6%.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,113.39 implies 8.21% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: S&P 500 Could Stumble to 6,500 in 2026, Warns Stifel
data.description:
Financial services company Stifel believes there is a 25% chance that the U.S. economy will enter a recession next year, which would bring the S&P 500 (SPX) to 6,500, implying downside of roughly 5% from current levels.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Stifel Chief Equity Strategist Barry Banister expects the benchmark index to trade in a range between 6,500 and 7,500 next year. The upper end of the range implies upside of 9%, which is "just the average annual S&P 500 price gain in the past 60 years."

Stifel Highlights Growth Potential but Flags Valuation and Labor Risks

Stifel's bull case points to EPS growth in the low teens and "one multiple of normal S&P 500 P/E compression."

However, the firm notes several risks, such as a weak labor market and elevated valuations. In addition, gross domestic product (GDP) could take a hit if Americans cut back on spending. To reduce risk, Stifel recommends hedging tech exposure with companies in the defensive growth and value sectors, such as healthcare, consumer staples, and gold.

data.title: Meta Gets Munched on Claims it is Allowing the Sale of Endangered Tiger Parts on Facebook
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) stock was flattened today as it was accused of allowing the sale of endangered species' parts on its social media platform Facebook.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Tigers, Sharks and Pangolins

According to an investigation by BBC News, tiger body parts, shark fins and pangolin scales are being offered for sale on Facebook.

The report highlighted videos of a tiger in a cage that was for sale as well as pictures of tiger bones and teeth, while another included two live tiger cubs in a box. An image showed a tiger head and bones for sale piled up on scales.

Five dead seahorses, including a pregnant male, were also on sale.

CITES, the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora, closely regulates international trade in species under threat.

The voluntary agreement, signed by more than 180 countries and other bodies, only allows the trade if it is both legal and sustainable.

Meta Rejects Practice

According to the WWF, tigers are on the brink of extinction. Just over a century ago, 100,000 wild tigers roamed across Asia. Today, approximately 5,600 live in a mere 5% of their historic range.

In a statement, Meta said: "We do not allow the sale of endangered species on our platforms."

The social media platform says it takes down that kind of material as soon as it becomes aware of it, saying it encourages users to "report any content they think may violate our policies".

However, it shows how vulnerable global social media networks are to illegal behavior as well as the additional issues with racist and sexist language, images and comments.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Planet Labs Stock (PL) Rockets 34% Higher After Its Latest Earnings
data.description:
The stock of Planet Labs (PL) is up 34% on Dec. 11 after the satellite Earth imaging company reported better-than-expected financial results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

For what was its fiscal third quarter, Planet Labs announced break-even earnings per share on sales of $81.3 million. Wall Street was looking for a loss from the company of $0.06 a share on revenue of $73.5 million. A year ago, Planet Labs reported a loss of $0.02 and revenue of $61.3 million.

The company also provided bullish forward guidance. Previous guidance called for fiscal year 2026 revenue of $281 million to $289 million. Now the midpoint of the company's guidance is $299 million. That implies fourth-quarter revenue of $78 million. Wall Street had forecast fiscal Q4 sales of $72 million.

To The Moon

Planet Labs management team said the company ended the quarter with cash and cash equivalents of almost $700 million. The strong results, guidance, and cash position have PL stock soaring. However, the big move higher is nothing new for the San Francisco-based start-up company.

PL stock jumped 48% higher after the company's previous quarterly print in September. Moves in Planet Labs stock can be exacerbated by high short interest. About 13% of PL shares available for trading are sold short, which is high and means that a lot of investors are betting that the share price will decline. The average short interest for an S&P 500-listed stock is about 4%.

Is PL Stock a Buy?

Planet Labs stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on seven Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average PL price target of $16.56 implies 1.66% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Broadcom Stock (AVGO) Slips as Earnings are 'Primed for Disappointment'
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) stock was down on Thursday after an investor in the semiconductor company gave a warning ahead of its Q4 2025 earnings report. Huntington National Bank manager of global growth equity strategy Peter Sorrentino told Bloomberg that Broadcom is "primed for disappointment" with its latest earnings report.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Sorrentino highlighted the strong performance of Broadcom stock over the past six months, which has rallied 61.27%. These gains have Sorrentino worried that the company's shares have climbed too high, and that investors' and analysts' expectations for Q4 are inflated. It's worth noting that Huntington National Bank hasn't increased its stake in AVGO during this price run. The investment bank still holds 1.16 million shares of AVGO stock worth $383.33 million, representing 0.87% of its portfolio.

Wall Street's expectations for Broadcom in Q4 2025 are high. Analysts are expecting the company to report adjusted earnings per share of $1.87 alongside revenue of $17.47 billion. For comparison, Broadcom reported adjusted EPS of $1.42 and revenue of $14.05 billion in Q4 2024. It has consistently beaten EPS and revenue estimates in the past eight quarters.

Broadcom Stock Movement Today

Broadcom stock was down 3.11% on Thursday but was still up 73.79% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 128.59% over the past 12 months. The artificial intelligence (AI) boom has been a driving factor behind the stock's 2025 growth, as it has increased the demand for server components manufactured by the company.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Tariff Revenue Unexpectedly Slips in November
data.description:
U.S. tariff revenue fell for the first time since President Trump announced his first tariff order in February, according to the U.S. Treasury Department's Monthly Treasury Statement.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

November's customs duties collection fell to $30.76 billion from $31.35 billion in October amid President Trump stepping back from his aggressive tariff approach. Earlier this month, he signed an executive order that excluded certain grocery items from reciprocal tariffs, such as coffee, tomatoes, oranges, and beef.

Trump Weighs More Tariff Carveouts as Rates Fall

Furthermore, Trump told Politico that he would consider additional tariff carveouts on consumer staple products as concerns about an affordability crisis grip Americans.

In an updated report, the Congressional Budget Office (CBO) expects the U.S. to generate $3 trillion in tariff revenue by 2030, down from its previous estimate of $4 trillion. The CBO cited Trump lowering tariffs on various countries as a major factor behind the drop, with the agency's estimate of the overall U.S. tariff rate falling to 14% from 18%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: SoundHound AI (SOUN) Teams Up With OpenTable for In-Car Voice Reservations
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) announced a new partnership with OpenTable to introduce a fully conversational, in-vehicle voice-powered reservations agent as part of its in-car voice commerce platform.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

For context, SoundHound AI focuses on voice recognition and natural language processing, providing AI-powered solutions for multiple industries. Meanwhile, OpenTable is an online restaurant reservation platform.

More Details About the Partnership

With this partnership, SoundHound lets drivers and passengers find, choose, and book restaurant reservations completely hands-free using natural voice commands through the car's infotainment system. Connected to OpenTable's network of more than 60,000 restaurants worldwide, the system checks real-time availability and secures bookings within seconds — turning what is typically a multi-step process into a seamless, voice-driven experience for people on the move.

Commenting on the partnership, Michael Zagorsek, COO of SoundHound AI, stated that this is another big step in the company's mission to bring voice-powered transactions into the car. He added that the voice commerce platform gives drivers an easier, more intuitive, and completely hands-free way to plan their dining experiences.

What This Means for SoundHound

The partnership with OpenTable strengthens SoundHound's push into voice-powered commerce and expands the real-world utility of its in-car AI platform.

By adding restaurant reservations to its list of voice-enabled features, SoundHound strengthens its position in the growing market for connected, AI-driven vehicles. The integration also helps the company build stronger partnerships with automakers and shows how voice technology can create new business opportunities through simple, frequent tasks that people do on the go.

Is SOUN a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with two Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average SoundHound stock price target is $17.20, suggesting a potential upside of 44% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak
